name: Icons
description: The brand's icon system standardizes on Material UI Icons for general-purpose iconography, supplemented by custom vehicle/fleet icons for domain-specific concepts Material doesn't cover.
keyRule: Material UI Icons is the base library — don't source icons from another set. This is the strongest existing bridge between the brand system and the product design system, since product components are already MUI-based.

pages.name: Material UI Icons (overview)
pages.description: States Material UI Icons as the standard library, with a downloadable icon list/set for use in marketing material.

pages.name: Material UI Icons (usage grid)
pages.description: A grid of the approved icon set with sizing/style guidance (outlined vs. filled, consistent stroke weight).

pages.name: Things to avoid
pages.description: Do/don't pairs — mismatched icon styles, inconsistent sizing, and off-brand icon sets are shown as incorrect.

pages.name: Icons and colour
pages.description: Rules for applying brand colour to icons — approved colour/background combinations, contrast requirements, and a 'correct vs. avoid' pairing similar to the Colour section's approach.

pages.name: Icons usage
pages.description: General placement/sizing guidance in applied contexts (cards, callouts).

pages.name: Unavailable icons
pages.description: A fallback formula for concepts Material UI doesn't have a direct icon for — combine two existing icons (e.g. a tool + a shield) to represent the missing concept, rather than commissioning or sourcing a custom icon ad hoc.

doThis: Source all icons from Material UI Icons.
Use the two-icon combination formula for concepts without a direct match, instead of introducing an off-brand custom icon.
Follow the specified colour/contrast rules when colouring icons.
dontDoThis: Don't mix in icons from other libraries.
Don't mix filled and outlined styles in the same context.
Don't apply colour combinations outside what's shown in 'Icons and colour'.
relationToProductTokens: Genuine, working alignment: the product component library is built on Material Components / MUI, so the same icon set applies on both sides. This is the one area where the brand and product systems should actively share a source rather than maintain parallel ones — worth referencing this file from any future icon-related product documentation instead of restating it.
openQuestions: Confirm whether the product codebase's icon usage (material-icons ligature names, per Button.doc.json) draws from the exact same Material Icons variant/version as this brand file's downloadable set.
